Set theory is the study of sets

Model theory studies the models of various formal theories
a theory is a set of formulas in a particular formal logic and signature
The set of all models of a particular theory is called an elementary class
Tarski established quantifier elimination for real-closed fields

studies the properties of computable functions and the Turing degrees

Generalized recursion theory extends the ideas of recursion theory to computations that are no longer necessarily finite
a decision problem or function problem is algorithmically unsolvable if there is no possible computable algorithm that returns the correct answer for all legal inputs to the problem

The algorithmic unsolvability of the problem was proved by Yuri Matiyasevich in 1970

annotations.label: Consistency of non-Euclidean geometry via spherical model
